verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a p24 family cargo receptor cycling between ER and Golgi as a type I single-pass TM protein. While its steady-state distribution is dominantly ER/Golgi, p24 family members participate in COPI/COPII vesicular trafficking and have documented minority pools reaching the plasma membrane via the secretory pathway, with the TM orientation placing the ectodomain on the outer leaflet when at the PM. No cell-surface-directed clinical program is established, and the dominant compartment is endomembrane. Cell-state induction not documented for this gene specifically; no lysosomal exocytosis role; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering; no narrow lineage restriction.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Gene-specific evidence for a TMED9 PM pool is weak; could justifiably be called endomembrane_resident/no if strict per-gene surface evidence is required.
